---
title: "Project Log — Onboard Damage Triage"
subtitle: "Living document, updated weekly. Not a finished write-up."
output: github_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```

## Reference (update as it changes)

**Problem.** Automated building-damage triage from satellite imagery for conflict/disaster zones, running onboard/edge to cut unnecessary downlink.

**Reports.** Damaged-building counts by class/AOI + bandwidth/energy savings vs. transmit-everything baseline. No cost-of-war economics.

**Architecture (current — will evolve):**

![Onboard damage triage architecture: image pair feeds a ResNet damage classifier, which routes through a confidence threshold to transmit, human review, or discard, all logged for periodic offline retraining](architecture.png)

No RL — evaluated, rejected (reintroduces the black-box trust problem, adds overengineering risk).

**Datasets.** xBD (pretraining) → fine-tuned on self-built conflict set (UNOSAT damage labels + matched Google Earth historical patches, multiple cities). See Week entries below for where this stands.

---

## Week 1

- Surveyed EO foundation models, onboard-AI precedent.
- RL evaluated for onboard triage — rejected.
- First architecture draft: CNN classifier only.

## Week 2

- Compared deforestation vs. conflict/disaster damage assessment as project topic — picked the latter.
- Adopted xBD as baseline dataset.

## Week 3

- Cross-checked against project brief — good fit as a reconnaissance use case.
- BIOMASS flagged as unsuitable sensor (P-band SAR, wrong task) — will justify optical/xBD imagery choice instead.
- Dropped cost-of-war economics from scope.
- Architecture finalised (diagram above).
- Dataset shortlist: xBD + UNOSAT/Google Earth fine-tuning set, benchmarked against Risso et al. 2024 (Mariupol) and Mueller et al. 2021 (Aleppo — cross-city generalisation warning, up to 29% accuracy drop).

## Week 4


---

<!--
Template for new entries:

## Week N

- What you did
- What you decided, and why
- What's next
-->
